scope: Mapper coverage gap report for modern survey-era backtest (2008-2024). Documentation only — does not modify the mapper spec.

terminology.moral_boundary_salience: Identity-driven appeal is read through moral-boundary salience and moral-boundary loading.
terminology.engagement: One-dimensional scalar separate from policy alignment. Never called activation.
terminology.PF_TRB: Legacy implementation field names; not active model concepts.

grade_legend.strong: Direct standard-battery measurement available with low uncertainty.
grade_legend.moderate: Mix of strong and proxy/derived sources; medium uncertainty.
grade_legend.partial: Strong only on a single source (typically ANES year-specific).
grade_legend.weak: Mostly proxy or derived; high uncertainty.
grade_legend.derived: All-source derivation only; no direct items.
grade_legend.near-absent: Mostly missing; only weak proxies in one source.
grade_legend.absent: No usable signal in any source.

hard_gaps.reason: No standard battery measures aesthetic-style preference. Cannot be inferred from issue items.
hard_gaps.expected_failure_in_C3_C4: Style-driven elections (2016/2020/2024 era-elevated) miss white-non-college and college-educated cells. C4 will tag as mapper_error+candidate_signature_error; underlying cause is flat AES posterior.

hard_gaps.reason: CES doesn't measure compromise tolerance directly. ANES has only weak proxies.
hard_gaps.expected_failure_in_C3_C4: Rare; usually small impact since COM doesn't strongly drive vote choice. Possible suburban-moderate cell misses in landslide years.

hard_gaps.reason: CES is silent on human-malleability worldview. ANES yields it as proxy only.
hard_gaps.expected_failure_in_C3_C4: White-college vs non-college misclassification on cultural-traditionalism axis.

hard_gaps.reason: Weakest of the 7 boundaries across all sources. Most often proxy/derived.
hard_gaps.expected_failure_in_C3_C4: Working-class / non-college / multi-racial-working-class cell failures, especially in realignment elections. C4 will tag mapper_error+vote_formula_error; usually the true cause is class-loading mis-resolution.

hard_gaps.reason: Risk of conflation with policy salience or partisan strength. Survey 'interest' items can be read as both turnout signal AND salience signal.
hard_gaps.expected_failure_in_C3_C4: Multiple subgroups missing turnout simultaneously, asymmetric by party. C4 will tag turnout_model_error+mapper_error (engagement).

individual_vs_geography_gaps.individual_measurement_fix_strategy: Add survey items, accept wider posteriors, build proxy bridges with stated uncertainty. ACS / Esri cannot fix individual measurement gaps.
poststratification_gaps: Subgroup population shares for the year being backtested
geography_gaps: State / county / block-group composition of synthesized electorate

do_not_overfit.principle: Mapper revisions should only happen when failure pattern is (a) consistent across multiple coalitions, (b) tied to a target with weak or derived coverage, and (c) NOT explained by candidate-coding audit flags or poststratification mismatch.
skip_revision_when: Single-cell miss in a year with a known candidate-coding flag.
Failure on a cell with high uncertainty floor (AES, COM, ONT_H).
Failure on a single subgroup while all others pass.
Failure with delta within 1.5x tolerance (minor severity).
Failure across many cells with same directional sign — that's a candidate-signature problem, not a mapper problem.

gap_priority_ranking: PRO (weak in CES; proxy in ANES)
